title: TaRL Africa Is Hiring: Country Regional Manager (Programs) – Kaduna, Nigeria | Apply by 3 March 2026
summary: TaRL Africa is expanding its leadership team in Nigeria and is now hiring a Country Regional Manager (Programs) to drive regional scale, government partnership, and high-quality implementation of the Teaching at the Right Level (TaRL) approach. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); If you are an experienced program leader with a strong background in education systems reform, government engagement, and multi-state program management, this is an opportunity to lead transformative change at scale. Position: Country Regional Manager, Programs Location: Kaduna, Nigeria Reports to: Country Director, TaRL Africa Nigeria About TaRL Africa TaRL Africa is hosted by Empower Learning Africa, a not-for-profit organization headquartered in Nairobi, Kenya, with teams across C te d Ivoire, Nigeria, and Zambia. The initiative was launched in 2019 as a joint venture between Pratham and J- PAL. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); TaRL Africa partners with governments and organizations across more than 15 Sub-Saharan African countries to accelerate children s foundational skill acquisition using the evidence-based Teaching at the Right Level methodology. Together with partners, the initiative has reached over five million children. Our vision is for every child across Africa to have strong foundational skills that unlock lifelong opportunity. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Role: Country Regional Manager (CRM) The Country Regional Manager (CRM) will lead and expand TaRL programming within a defined region in Nigeria. This role oversees implementation across multiple states, manages regional teams, strengthens partnerships with state governments, and ensures program delivery with fidelity, scale, and sustainability. As a key member of the Country Management Team, you will shape regional strategy, drive operational excellence, oversee budgets, and lead continuous program improvement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); This position requires extensive travel within the region and occasional travel across Nigeria and Africa. Key Responsibilities 1. Program Leadership Partnership Management (40%) Strengthen technical implementation of the Teaching at the Right Level model. Ensure high-quality program delivery through data-informed decision-making. Provide oversight to state teams to maintain financial health and operational efficiency. Guide annual planning cycles and conduct periodic program reviews. Build strong, productive relationships with government stakeholders and community partners. Drive contextual program design aligned with regional needs. 2. Regional Strategy Scale (30%) Identify and execute strategic expansion opportunities across states. Promote government ownership and long-term sustainability. Translate regional priorities into coordinated work plans and budgets. Use needs assessments, stakeholder insights, and program data to refine strategy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 3. Strengthening the TaRL Ecosystem (15%) Improve operational tools, systems, and accountability frameworks. Lead special innovation projects in collaboration with Programme Design, Pedagogy, and Monitoring, Learning Evaluation teams. Contribute to cross-state learning and organizational knowledge sharing. 4. Team Leadership Performance Management (15%) Build, mentor, and manage high-performing regional teams. Set clear performance goals and monitor progress. Foster accountability, ownership, and continuous professional development. Cultivate a collaborative and impact-driven team culture. Who We Are Looking For We are seeking a results-oriented leader with: Proven experience managing medium to large-scale education or development programs Strong stakeholder engagement experience with governments and donors Demonstrated ability to lead multi-state implementation Strategic planning and expansion expertise Budget oversight and financial management capability Strong analytical and data-driven decision-making skills Excellent written and verbal communication skills ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Minimum Qualifications Bachelor s degree in Public Policy, International Development, Education, or related field (Master s degree such as MPA or MPP preferred). At least six (6) years of experience managing medium to large-scale programs. Experience working with donors, policymakers, researchers, and government agencies. Strong proficiency in Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Google Suite. Excellent communication skills. Why Join TaRL Africa? This is more than a job. It is a leadership opportunity to shape foundational learning outcomes for millions of children across Nigeria. You will: Influence education reform at scale Strengthen government systems for sustainable impact Lead high-performing teams across multiple states Contribute to a growing, mission-driven organization operating across Africa Play a key role in evidence-based education transformation ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); If you are ready to lead with purpose and deliver measurable impact, we encourage you to apply. Application Deadline: 3rd March 2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this job opportunity, click here . For more opportunities on jobs , click here . The post TaRL Africa Is Hiring: Country Regional Manager (Programs) – Kaduna, Nigeria | Apply by 3 March 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 2026 MILEAD Fellows Program for Young African Women Leaders (Applications Open)
summary: Application Deadline: Friday, March 20, 2026 Applications are now open for the 2026 MILEAD Fellows Program , a leadership development initiative designed to support and empower young African women leaders committed to driving positive change across the continent. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); For over a decade, the MILEAD Fellows Program has equipped emerging leaders with the knowledge, skills, and networks needed to challenge inequality, advance women s leadership, and implement community-driven solutions. About the MILEAD Fellows Program The MILEAD (Moremi Initiative Leadership and Empowerment Development) Fellows Program is a long-term leadership development initiative focused on identifying and nurturing promising young African women leaders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The program aims to: Build leadership capacity among young African women Strengthen knowledge, skills, and values for 21st-century leadership Create a supportive ecosystem for women s leadership across Africa Connect emerging leaders with influential African mentors and changemakers Each year, 25 outstanding young women are selected based on their leadership potential and commitment to making a difference in their communities or professional fields. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Program Structure and Key Features The program combines leadership training, mentoring, experiential learning, and community engagement. Key components include: A three-week residential Summer Institute in Ghana Intensive leadership and personal development training Mentorship from established African leaders Access to the MILEAD leadership network Ongoing support and lifelong peer solidarity ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); A distinguishing feature of the program is the MiCHANGE Community Project , where each fellow designs and leads a community-based initiative addressing issues affecting women and girls. Eligibility Criteria Applicants should meet the following requirements: Be a young African woman aged 19 25 Demonstrate leadership potential or experience Show commitment to addressing issues affecting women and girls in Africa Be interested in developing transformational leadership skills Be available to participate in the three-week residential Summer Institute in Ghana ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Selected candidates may be full-time students or professionals during the program, except during the residential institute period. Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Completed application form Updated CV Two recommendation letters ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); All application materials must be submitted via email. Email: Fellows@moremiinitiative.org Applicants are strongly encouraged to carefully review the program and application guidelines before submitting. Application Resources Download the full 2026 Application Package ( Application Pack | Recommendation-Form ), and share this opportunity with other inspiring emerging African women leaders in your network. Stay updated: follow us @MoremiAfrica #MILEADFellows #MILEAD2026 EMAIL: Fellows@moremiinitiative.org | TEL: +233 530 429 644 Why Apply? The MILEAD Fellows Program is designed to build a pipeline of African women leaders equipped to drive systemic change. Fellows benefit from: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); High-quality leadership training Practical experience through community projects Exposure to experienced African leaders and mentors A lifelong network of women leaders across Africa The program emphasizes long-term impact by integrating leadership development with real-world community engagement. Contact and Updates For more information: Website: www.moremiinitiative.org Facebook: /MoremiAfrica Twitter/X: @MoremiAfrica Email: Fellows@moremiinitiative.org Tel: +233 530 429 644 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The 2026 MILEAD Fellows Program offers an important opportunity for young African women who are ready to strengthen their leadership skills and contribute meaningfully to social change across the continent. Interested candidates should submit their applications before March 20, 2026 . Find out more https://moremiinitiative.org/2026-milead-fellows-program-announcement/ The post 2026 MILEAD Fellows Program for Young African Women Leaders (Applications Open) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FAO EYPA Mentorship Journey Launches Phase II to Empower Young Women Farmers Across Africa
summary: The Empowerment of Young Women Farmers in Africa through Inclusive Mentorship Programme (EYPA) is officially launching Phase II , marking a major step forward in supporting young women in agriculture across Africa. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Building on the success of its pilot phase, the programme is being implemented through a renewed partnership between the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) and the Pan-African Farmers Organization (PAFO) . This new phase places a strong focus on young women farmers , aiming to strengthen leadership, entrepreneurship, and economic opportunities within Africa s agrifood systems. What is EYPA Phase II? The first phase of the EYPA Programme s Mentorship Programme is designed to provide inclusive mentorship, capacity building, and professional networking opportunities for young women working in agriculture. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Phase II will scale up the successful mentorship model developed during the pilot stage by addressing gender-specific challenges that often limit women s participation and leadership in agrifood systems. Through this initiative, participants will gain: Tailored mentorship from experienced professionals Capacity-building activities and training sessions Networking opportunities across regions Skills development for agribusiness growth Increased confidence and leadership capacity ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The programme also supports the goals of the International Year of the Woman Farmer (IYWF 2026) by promoting women s economic empowerment and closing gender gaps in agriculture. Call for Expression of Interest Now Open A Call for Expression of Interest has been launched to recruit both mentors and mentees for the programme. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Application Deadline: Friday, 6 March 2026 Find the call for mentees here Find the call for mentors here Up to 50 young women farmers from different regions across Africa will be selected to participate in this phase. What Participants Will Gain EYPA Phase II aims to unlock the potential of young women leaders and innovators by equipping them with practical and business-focused skills, including: Financial literacy and business planning Organizational and leadership skills Market access and value chain understanding Agribusiness development strategies Professional mentorship and peer learning ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); By strengthening these competencies, the programme contributes to the broader transformation of Africa s agrifood systems and supports sustainable economic growth. Regional Pitching Sessions and Final Competition Following the structure of the pilot phase, the mentorship journey will culminate in regional pitching sessions where mentees will present their agribusiness ideas and business models. Participants will showcase: Business and financial models Innovation and sustainability approaches Social and economic impact of their ventures ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Finalists demonstrating strong pitching skills will advance to a final pitching competition hosted at the PAFO Headquarters in Kigali, Rwanda . During the final event, selected mentees will: Present their agribusiness progress Showcase outcomes from the mentorship journey Compete for two cash prizes Strengthening Youth and Women Leadership in Agriculture The EYPA Mentorship Programme reflects the shared commitment of FAO and PAFO to building youth leadership and empowering women in agriculture. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Implemented under a broader Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) collaboration, Phase II represents a strategic effort to equip young women farmers with the tools, knowledge, and opportunities needed to thrive in modern agrifood systems. By investing in mentorship and capacity development, EYPA aims to nurture the next generation of agricultural leaders driving innovation and sustainability across Africa. The launch of EYPA Phase II signals a powerful opportunity for young women farmers to strengthen their skills, grow their agribusinesses, and expand their professional networks. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); For aspiring women leaders in agriculture, this programme offers a unique platform to learn, connect, and showcase innovation on a continental stage. The post FAO EYPA Mentorship Journey Launches Phase II to Empower Young Women Farmers Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: LinkedIn Future of Work Fund 2026: Global AI Workforce Grant Supporting Nonprofits Preparing Young Adults for the AI Economy
summary: The LinkedIn Future of Work Fund 2026 is a global philanthropic grant initiative designed to help nonprofit organizations prepare young people for a rapidly changing labor market shaped by artificial intelligence. As automation and AI technologies continue transforming industries, LinkedIn is expanding its commitment to economic inclusion by funding organizations that help young adults successfully transition into an AI-powered workforce. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The 2026 edition builds on LinkedIn s first year of funding nonprofit innovators worldwide and introduces an expanded funding round combining financial grants and in-kind support resources. The program focuses specifically on young adults facing structural barriers to employment and aims to ensure that technological transformation does not widen inequality. Why the Future of Work Fund Matters Artificial intelligence is not just creating new jobs; it is reshaping nearly every existing one. According to LinkedIn projections, approximately 70% of the skills required for jobs will change by 2030. This means traditional career preparation models are becoming outdated. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The challenge is not simply training people to use technology. Instead, the workforce now requires a combination of: AI fluency and digital capability Critical thinking and adaptability Communication and collaboration Problem-solving in uncertain environments ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Many young adults particularly those from underserved backgrounds lack access to training and opportunities that help them adapt to this shift. The Future of Work Fund exists to support organizations solving exactly this problem. What the Fund Supports The fund provides both financial grants and in-kind support to nonprofit organizations delivering programs that expand economic opportunity in an AI-driven labor market. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Key Focus Areas Organizations applying must demonstrate a clear and practical approach to at least one of the following: 1. Preparing Young Adults for AI-Driven Careers Programs that teach AI literacy, digital skills, and workforce readiness for modern job markets. 2. Improving Job Access Initiatives helping career starters connect with employment opportunities using technology and AI-enabled pathways. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 3. Supporting Workforce Success Training that helps young workers thrive after employment through adaptability, communication, and lifelong learning skills. 4. System-Level Workforce Innovation Projects influencing how education systems, training institutions, or employers integrate AI workforce preparation. What LinkedIn Means by AI Innovation LinkedIn is not only interested in teaching technical coding skills. Instead, the fund prioritizes organizations that combine technological learning with human capabilities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Supported initiatives typically include: AI-fluency training programs Career navigation tools powered by AI Skills-matching platforms Job readiness pathways Human-skill development (communication, collaboration, adaptability) Alternative credential programs Who the Fund Aims to Help The program focuses on young adults entering the workforce who face employment barriers. These may include: Individuals from low-income communities First-generation professionals Learners outside traditional university pathways Underemployed youth Career starters without professional networks ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Typical target age range: 18 24 (varies by country) Eligibility Requirements for Organizations To qualify, applicants must meet all of the following conditions. Organizational Status Must be a legally registered nonprofit, charity, or equivalent not-for-profit organization U.S. organizations must hold 501(c)(3) status Must be able to directly receive grant funding ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Program Focus Organizations must demonstrate: Workforce development programming Clear use of AI innovation OR preparation for AI workforce transition Support for young adults facing economic opportunity barriers Organizations not meeting these requirements are encouraged to use LinkedIn s free nonprofit resources instead. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Funding and Support Structure Selected organizations receive a combination of monetary and strategic assistance. Financial Support Cash grant funding for program implementation and expansion. In-Kind Support Access to LinkedIn expertise Workforce insights and data Platform resources Strategic guidance This hybrid model helps organizations scale impact beyond traditional grants. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Evaluation Criteria Applications are reviewed based on: Demonstrated impact potential Clarity of AI integration Capacity to support underserved young adults Scalability of program model Alignment with economic inclusion goals Application Deadline March 15, 2026 at 12 PM PT Submit your application HERE . Visit the LinkedIn Social Impact website HERE for more details. Click HERE to discover more grant opportunities. The post LinkedIn Future of Work Fund 2026: Global AI Workforce Grant Supporting Nonprofits Preparing Young Adults for the AI Economy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: International Law Fellows Network 2026 on the Future of the Rule of Law
summary: The global rule of law is under unprecedented strain. Long-standing models of legal cooperation and governance once shaped by post Cold War optimism are now being challenged by political polarisation, populism, and competing global visions of governance. In response, leading institutions are convening the next generation of legal professionals to rethink how the rule of law can be defended and strengthened in the 21st century. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The International Law Fellows Network 2026: The Future of the Rule of Law in a Fractured World offers a unique opportunity for early- to mid-career lawyers to engage in this urgent conversation and develop practical strategies for safeguarding justice, accountability, and legal integrity worldwide. Why the Rule of Law Matters Now The United States and Europe have historically promoted rule-of-law frameworks through foreign aid, technical assistance, and policy conditionality. However, those approaches are now facing mounting pressure: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); In the United States , political attacks on courts and constitutional structures reveal internal vulnerabilities. Across Europe, democratic institutions are being tested by populism, executive overreach, and disinformation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Globally, rival governance models from Russia and China emphasize sovereignty and centralized control over accountability and individual rights. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); As these trends reshape global governance, rising legal professionals must grapple with how to uphold core principles fairness, equality, transparency, and justice in an increasingly complex world. About the Programme This high-level programme is convened by Salzburg Global Seminar , the International Bar Association (IBA) , and the 21st Century Trust . It brings together early- to mid-career lawyers working in: Major law firms International organizations Public institutions Human rights and advocacy spaces ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Participants will explore how their work in cross-border transactions, compliance, dispute resolution, and human rights law can help defend and adapt the rule of law in a fractured geopolitical landscape. Programme Details Programme Dates: June 11 14, 2026 Location: Schloss Leopoldskron , Salzburg, Austria Application Deadline: April 5, 2026 Fees Funding Fully Funded: Participants selected through IBA member firms or the Salzburg Global Cutler Fellowship community will receive full coverage of programme fees, accommodation, and meals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); IBA-supported participants may also receive travel funding. General Applicants: Programme cost: $4,250 Employer sponsorship is encouraged. Who Should Apply? Ideal Participants Early- to mid-career lawyers with an interest in international law and global governance ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Professionals engaged in cross-border legal practice or policy work Advocates committed to upholding democratic institutions and human rights Programme Goals The International Law Fellows Network aims to: Strengthen leadership among rising international lawyers Equip participants to defend and adapt rule-of-law frameworks Foster transatlantic collaboration on global legal challenges Promote legal systems grounded in accountability, fairness, and rights Key Questions the Programme Will Explore How can lawyers safeguard the rule of law amid geopolitical fragmentation? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); What role do legal professionals play in defending democratic institutions? How can global legal frameworks respond to competing governance models? What practical strategies can strengthen transparency, accountability, and justice worldwide? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); How to Apply Click HERE , To apply. Deadline: April 5, 2026 In a time when legal norms and democratic institutions are increasingly challenged, the need for principled and forward-thinking legal professionals has never been greater. The International Law Fellows Network 2026 offers a powerful platform for rising lawyers to shape the future of global justice and ensure the rule of law remains a cornerstone of free, fair, and stable societies. If you are ready to contribute to this mission, apply today and become part of a global network committed to defending the rule of law. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); For more Fellowship opportunities, click HERE . The post Apply Now: International Law Fellows Network 2026 on the Future of the Rule of Law appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
